phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the wave's edge and his face in one arc — the crescent farms and pokes at once; learn the curve's sweet spot.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W shield-trade window: orbs absorb his poke and pay it back on the three-hit passive.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — orb-dash crash combos edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your orb-dash crash combos beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your orb-dash crash combos beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight edge on first item: your orb-dash crash combos beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Diana dives: E the carry through the front line, R the clump that collapses, and reset through the wreckage.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Your orb-dash crash combos out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at level 1 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W shield-trade window: orbs absorb his poke and pay it back on the three-hit passive.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E pull joins... no — your dash threat begins: Q-mark into E reset chases are live; trade on every landed crescent.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your orb-dash crash combos beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Moonfall online — the R pull turns any clumped trade into your fight. With jungle help it's a guaranteed flip.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item your orb-dash crash combos beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.why: Late Diana dives: E the carry through the front line, R the clump that collapses, and reset through the wreckage.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W shield-trade window: orbs absorb his poke and pay it back on the three-hit passive.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your orb-dash crash combos beat Lux's snare-shield poke combos on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — orb-dash crash combos edge out the snare-shield poke combos when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your orb-dash crash combos beat Lux's snare-shield poke combos on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean your way — orb-dash crash combos edge out the snare-shield poke combos when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight edge at two-plus items: your orb-dash crash combos beat Lux's snare-shield poke combos on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the wave's edge and his face in one arc — the crescent farms and pokes at once; learn the curve's sweet spot.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W shield-trade window: orbs absorb his poke and pay it back on the three-hit passive.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Vex's gloom-fear anti-dash zones own this window — your orb-dash crash combos don't answer them at level 3 yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Respect window: through levels 4-5 the gloom-fear anti-dash zones beat your orb-dash crash combos cleanly. Trade only off Vex's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Moonfall online — the R pull turns any clumped trade into your fight. With jungle help it's a guaranteed flip.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Vex's gloom-fear anti-dash zones edge out your orb-dash crash combos on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your orb-dash crash combos beat the shroud-energy burst windows in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Akali answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Your orb-dash crash combos out-muscle Akali's shroud-energy burst windows at level 2 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E pull joins... no — your dash threat begins: Q-mark into E reset chases are live; trade on every landed crescent.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your orb-dash crash combos beat Akali's shroud-energy burst windows on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Neither side owns this window: the shroud-energy burst windows and your orb-dash crash combos cancel out at the level-6 breakpoint. The HP bars walking into it decide who presses first.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: the mark-dash combo chunks half bars and the reset chains through waves.

phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean your way — orb-dash crash combos edge out the wave-dash blade stacks when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the wave's edge and his face in one arc — the crescent farms and pokes at once; learn the curve's sweet spot.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W shield-trade window: orbs absorb his poke and pay it back on the three-hit passive.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E pull joins... no — your dash threat begins: Q-mark into E reset chases are live; trade on every landed crescent.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Mark-dash trades on cooldown — Q, E to the mark, passive three-hit, walk. The lane respects the curve now.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your orb-dash crash combos beat Pantheon's point-click spear bullying on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean your way — orb-dash crash combos edge out the point-click spear bullying when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Your orb-dash crash combos out-muscle Pantheon's point-click spear bullying at two-plus items — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the wave's edge and his face in one arc — the crescent farms and pokes at once; learn the curve's sweet spot.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W shield-trade window: orbs absorb his poke and pay it back on the three-hit passive.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight deficit at level 3: Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving edge out your orb-dash crash combos on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Mark-dash trades on cooldown — Q, E to the mark, passive three-hit, walk. The lane respects the curve now.

phases.label: Level 6

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the windwall dash-weaving out-tick your orb-dash crash combos in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Diana dives: E the carry through the front line, R the clump that collapses, and reset through the wreckage.

phases.label: Level 1

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W shield-trade window: orbs absorb his poke and pay it back on the three-hit passive.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight deficit at level 3: Yone's double-blade spirit dives edge out your orb-dash crash combos on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Mark-dash trades on cooldown — Q, E to the mark, passive three-hit, walk. The lane respects the curve now.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Moonfall online — the R pull turns any clumped trade into your fight. With jungle help it's a guaranteed flip.
